economic_notes: Ontario First Nation Limited Partnership distributions of $843,905. Greenstone Gold Mine revenue of $917,577. First Nations Drinking Water Settlement revenue of $202,358. New CMHC housing loan of $919,000 obtained subsequent to year end. Matawa First Nation Management revenue of $337,908. Nishnawbe Aski Nation revenue of $273,655.
notes: Audit is qualified on three bases: (1) inability to verify portfolio investments in various entities; (2) no consolidated budget prepared as required by PS 1200; (3) PS 3280 Asset Retirement Obligations not implemented - management has not completed assessment of tangible capital assets for potential AROs. Five subsidiaries consolidated, all inactive. Honoraria of $241,435 recorded (likely council remuneration) but no formal schedule of remuneration provided. CMHC housing loans secured by 7 and 8 housing units respectively (15 total). Deferred revenue increased significantly from $10.9M to $15.3M, largely due to Infrastructure Ontario ($2.78M) and ISC ($11.39M). One-time Greenstone Gold Mine execution payment of $1,000 per capita distribution to members in 2020 - residual amounts for minors still held.
warnings: Qualified audit opinion issued — financial statements may not fully reflect true position
